title: Which?
content:
آ آ آ آ So, the three Court-ladies began
آ آ آ آ Their trial of who judged best
آ آ آ آ In esteeming the love of a man:
آ آ آ آ Who preferred with most reason was thereby confessed
آ آ آ آ Boy-Cupidï؟½s exemplary catcher and cager;
آ آ آ آ An Abbï؟½ crossed legs to decide on the wager.

آ آ آ آ First the Duchesse: ï؟½Mine for me,
آ آ آ آ Who were it but Godï؟½s for Him,
آ آ آ آ And the Kingï؟½s for who but he?
آ آ آ آ Both faithful and loyal, one grace more shall brim
آ آ آ آ His cup with perfection: a ladyï؟½s true lover,
آ آ آ آ He holds, save his God and his king, none above her.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ ï؟½I requireï؟½, outspoke the Marquise,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Pure thoughts, ay, but also fine deeds:
آ آ آ آ Play the paladin must he, to please
آ آ آ آ My whim, and, to prove my knightï؟½s service exceeds
آ آ آ آ Your saintï؟½s and your loyalistï؟½s praying and kneeling,
آ آ آ آ Show wounds, each wide mouth to my mercy appealing.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Then the Comtesse: ï؟½My choice be a wretch,
آ آ آ آ Mere losel in body and soul,
آ آ آ آ Thrice accurst! What care I, so he stretch
آ آ آ آ Arms to me his sole savior, loveï؟½s ultimate goal,
آ آ آ آ Out of earth and menï؟½s noise, names of ï؟½infidel,ï؟½ ï؟½traitor.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Cast up at him? Crown me, crownï؟½s adjudicator!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ And the Abbï؟½ uncrossed his legs,
آ آ آ آ Took snuff, a reflective pinch,
آ آ آ آ Broke silence: ï؟½The question begs
آ آ آ آ Much pondering ere I pronounce. Shall I flinch?
آ آ آ آ The love which to one and one only has reference
آ آ آ آ Seems terribly like what perhaps gains Godï؟½s preference.ï؟½
meaning: About: Robert Browning was a British poet and playwright whose mastery of dramatic verse, especially dramatic monologues, made him one of the foremost Victorian poets.
